channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Biden will decide on US Steel acquisition after influential panel fails to reach consensus
item.description: A powerful government panel has failed to reach consensus on the possible national security risks of a nearly $15 billion proposed deal for Nippon Steel of Japan to purchase U.S. Steel.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak, Marc Levy And Ashraf Khalil, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Bill Clinton is hospitalized with a fever but in good spirits, spokesperson says
item.description: The 78-year-old former president was hospitalized “for testing and observation” after developing a fever, his deputy chief of staff said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Bill Clinton is hospitalized with a fever but in good spirits, spokesperson says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Former President Bill Clinton was admitted Monday to MedStar Georgetown University Hospital in Washington after developing a fever. The 78-year-old was hospitalized in the “afternoon for testing and observation," Angel Urena, Clinton's deputy chief of staff, said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge rules Arkansas law allowing criminal charges against librarians is unconstitutional
item.description: A federal judge has struck down key parts of an Arkansas law that would have allowed criminal charges against librarians and booksellers for providing so-called “harmful” materials to minors.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Congress looks to ban Chinese drones widely used in US: What to know about the debate
item.description: Lawmakers in Washington are seeking to ban new sales of drones from two dominant Chinese manufacturers, arguing they could be used to spy on Americans and that the low-cost models are hurting the U.S. drone industry.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: House report accuses Matt Gaetz of paying women for sex, using illegal drugs, accepting improper gifts
item.description: Former Rep. Matt Gaetz, R-Fla., allegedly paid multiple women for sex and illicit drugs, according to a House Ethics Committee report.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: As Texas Chief Justice Nathan Hecht prepares to retire, he reflects on the Supreme Court he helped change
item.description: Over 35 years, Hecht modernized the court, increased access to justice for the poor and saw his conservative views come to dominate the bench.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Eleanor Klibanoff, KPRC 2

item.title: New York Gov. Hochul orders prison staffers involved in inmate's deadly beating to be fired
item.description: New York Gov. Kathy Hochul has ordered the firing of more than a dozen prison staffers over a beating that resulted in the death of an inmate earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Watchdog releases report highlighting the worst ethics violations it saw from public officials in 2024
item.description: The Foundation for Accountability and Civic Trust (FACT), a conservative-leaning ethics watchdog, released a year-end round up of 2024's worst ethics violations committed by public officials that the group investigated this year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Review by Senate Democrats finds more unreported luxury trips by Clarence Thomas
item.description: A report by Democrats on the Judiciary Committee found additional travel taken in 2021 by Thomas but not reported on his annual financial disclosure, including trips on private jets and a yacht trip.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Flood gates are dropped from a plan to protect the Jersey Shore’s back bays from catastrophic storms
item.description: Huge gates at the mouths of three inlets and barriers across bays are no longer part of a plan to protect New Jersey’s back bays from the type of catastrophic flooding they endured during Superstorm Sandy.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: What Trump's decision to wade into spending fight tells us about the next 4 years
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — After days of threats and demands, Donald Trump had little to show for it once lawmakers passed a budget deal in the early hours of Saturday, narrowly averting a pre-Christmas government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, STEPHEN GROVES, JILL COLVIN and JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ocasio-Cortez faces uncertain political future
item.description: Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez (D-N.Y.) is facing an uncertain future after a pivotal setback this week, when she lost her race to lead Democrats on the House Oversight and Accountability Committee. The 74-year-old Rep. Gerry Connolly’s (D-Va.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Trudo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lara Trump says she's removing herself from consideration to be Florida senator
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Lara Trump, the daughter-in-law of President-elect Donald Trump, said Saturday that she's removing herself from consideration to be a Florida senator — ending speculation that she could replace Republican Sen.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump selects ex-DHS official for key DOJ post
item.description: He was previously an acting general counsel and chief of staff at DHS. Before that role, Mizelle was the counsel to the deputy attorney general at the DOJ.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Senate review of Supreme Court ethics finds more luxury trips and urges enforceable code of conduct
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A nearly two-year investigation by Democratic senators of Supreme Court ethics details more luxury travel by Justice Clarence Thomas and urges Congress to establish a way to enforce a new code of conduct.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden signs bill that averts a government shutdown and brings a close to days of Washington upheaval
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden signed a bill into law Saturday that averts a government shutdown, bringing a final close to days of upheaval after Congress approved a temporary funding plan just past the deadline and refused President-elect Donald Trump’s core debt demands in the package.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, FARNOUSH AMIRI and MATT BROWN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Funding saga forecasts future struggles for Speaker Johnson
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) averted a government shutdown, but the days-long rollercoaster as the leaders cycled through multiple plans leaves serious questions about how the Speaker will manage the House GOP under President-elect Trump — and whether he can keep his gavel.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Here are the 34 Republicans that voted against the funding bill to avoid shutdown
item.description: More than 30 House Republicans voted against the government funding bill to avert a shutdown on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Musk helped kill a congressional spending bill. But much of what he spread was misinformation
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump's billionaire ally Elon Musk played a key role this week in killing a bipartisan funding proposal that would have prevented a government shutdown, railing against the plan in a torrent of more than 100 X posts that included multiple false claims.
item.dc:creator: MELISSA GOLDIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A judge says Missouri's abortion ban isn’t enforceable, but there's no start date for abortions
item.description: JEFFERSON CITY, Mo. (AP) — A judge ruled Friday that Missouri’s near-total abortion ban is unenforceable under a new constitutional amendment, though Planned Parenthood said the decision is still not sufficient for it to resume providing abortions in the state.
item.dc:creator: DAVID A. LIEB, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: House passes bill to avert government shutdown after whirlwind funding fight
item.description: The House approved legislation to avert a government shutdown hours before the deadline Friday, sending the bill to the Senate for consideration after a whirlwind week on Capitol Hill.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, Fox 5

item.title: Democrats say lines of communication open with GOP on funding, but no bill presented
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.) told his party that lines of communication have reopened between his office and Speaker Mike Johnson’s (R-La.) to avert a government shutdown this weekend, members said.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota House tie in peril after judge rules DFL lawmaker can’t be sworn in
item.description: A district court judge on Friday granted a challenge to Democratic Rep.-elect Curtis Johnson’s residency, blocking him from taking office.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson and Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: House rejects Trump-backed plan on government shutdown, leaving next steps uncertain
item.description: A day before a potential government shutdown, the House resoundingly rejected President-elect Donald Trump's new plan to fund operations and suspend the debt ceiling, as Democrats and dozens of Republicans refused to accommodate his sudden demands.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO and KEVIN FREKING Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Global warming can’t be ignored, Montana’s top court says, upholding landmark climate case
item.description: Montana’s Supreme Court has upheld a landmark climate ruling that said the state was violating residents’ constitutional right to a clean environment by permitting oil, gas and coal projects without regard for global warming.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Federal Reserve cuts its key rate by a quarter-point but envisions fewer reductions next year
item.description: The Federal Reserve cut its key interest rate by a quarter-point — its third cut this year — but also signaled that it expects to reduce rates more slowly next year than it previously envisioned, largely because of still-elevated inflation.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Senate passes defense bill that will raise troop pay and aims to counter China's power
item.description: The Senate has passed a defense bill that authorize significant pay raises for junior enlisted service members and boost overall military spending to $895 billion. It also strips coverage of transgender medical treatments for children of military members.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: In last-ditch effort, Democrats push to get Julie Su confirmed as labor secretary
item.description: Acting Secretary Julie Su has led the Labor Department for nearly two years, despite never getting a Senate confirmation vote. With time running out, her staunchest supporters haven't given up.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu, MPR News

item.title: The president of the NCAA calls for a ban on 'prop bets' in college sports
item.description: As sports betting has spread across the U.S., college athletes have increasingly faced harassment from bettors. A federal ban on wagers that concern individual performances could help, the NCAA says.
item.dc:creator: Becky Sullivan, MPR News

item.title: Trump tariff plan threatens to drive up gas prices
item.description: Trump said that on his first day in office, he would place 25 percent tariffs on all Canadian and Mexican goods. The two countries are the leading sources of U.S. oil imports.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, WFLA, NBC
